name: Preserved Lemons
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the lemons by quartering them from the top to within half an inch of the bottom.
2. Sprinkle salt on the exposed flesh and reshape the fruit.
3. Place 1 tablespoon of salt at the bottom of a sterilized one-pint mason jar.
4. Pack in the lemons, pressing them down to release juices and make room for more.
5. Add optional spices between layers if desired.
6. Ensure the juice covers all lemons; add fresh lemon juice if necessary (not chemically produced).
7. Leave some air space before sealing the jar.
8. Store the jar in a warm place, shaking it daily to distribute salt and juice for 30 days.
9. Rinse the preserved lemons under running water as needed, removing pulp if desired.
10. Use within up to one year; the pickling liquid can be used two or three times over the course of a year.
According to Michael Field, extracting maximum juice: boil the lemon in water for 2-3 minutes before squeezing.
